message: The budget is checked only after job_func executes. After one run under max_runs(3), changing the same job to max_runs(1) and invoking run_all() executes the function a second time before cancellation. This violates last-call-wins and the invariant that the (n+1)-th execution never happens.

The counter increments only after job_func returns. If a max_runs(1) function raises, it was called but _run_count remains 0 and the job remains scheduled; a second scheduler invocation calls it again. This violates the requirement to count when Job.run() actually calls job_func and permits an (n+1)-th execution.

Job.run's return documentation says CancelJob is returned when the deadline is reached, but run-budget exhaustion now also returns CancelJob. The public method documentation no longer describes all cancellation outcomes.
schema-v3 generated trivial/medium runs require completed SURFACE_CLOSE or its canonical automatic Claude-unavailable skip before VERIFY merge.
